  • Hide All Code

Reproducible research

Arseniy Vanitskiy

13.04.2026

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)

1 Wymagane pakiety

library(tm)           # Przetwarzanie tekstu
library(SnowballC)    # Stemming
library(cluster)      # Klastrowanie
library(wordcloud)    # Chmury słów
library(factoextra)   # Wizualizacje klastrów
library(RColorBrewer) # Kolory
library(ggplot2)      # Wykresy
library(dplyr)        # Przetwarzanie danych
library(ggrepel)      # Etykiety na wykresach
library(DT)           # Interaktywne tabele

2 Dane tekstowe

2.1 Wczytanie dokumentów z folderu

# Ustaw w razie potrzeby poprawną ścieżkę do folderu
docs <- DirSource("textfolder")

# Przykład ścieżki bezwzględnej:
# docs <- DirSource("C:/User/Documents/textfolder")

corpus <- VCorpus(docs)

2.2 Podgląd korpusu

inspect(corpus)
## <<VCorpus>>
## Metadata:  corpus specific: 0, document level (indexed): 0
## Content:  documents: 8
## 
## [[1]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 7838
## 
## [[2]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 5642
## 
## [[3]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 3916
## 
## [[4]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 2735
## 
## [[5]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 6666
## 
## [[6]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 3668
## 
## [[7]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 3525
## 
## [[8]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 4294
corpus[[1]]
## <<PlainTextDocument>>
## Metadata:  7
## Content:  chars: 7838
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "Yuichi is searching for a man who pushed his son, in the first-class compartment of the bullet train, but he finds Prince instead and is tasered by her."                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
## [2] ""                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
## [3] "Ladybug steals the briefcase from Lemon and Tangerine but is attacked by another assassin (this time from Mexico), the \"Wolf\" (Benito A. Martínez Ocasio) (a Mexican assassin and former kingpin of a drug cartel), who blames Ladybug for fatally poisoning his entire wedding party, including his newlywed wife (whom Ladybug had actually saved). Ladybug was about to get off the train at the next station, but was met by Wolf at the door, who attacks Ladybug. Ladybug doesn't recognize the Wolf and denies having poisoned his entire wedding party. But Ladybug was present at the party as a waiter."
corpus[[1]][2]
## $meta
##   author       : character(0)
##   datetimestamp: 2026-04-18 23:32:26.4809589385986
##   description  : character(0)
##   heading      : character(0)
##   id           : Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt
##   language     : en
##   origin       : character(0)

3 Przetwarzanie i oczyszczanie tekstu

3.1 Normalizacja i usunięcie zbędnych znaków

# Zapewnienie kodowania UTF-8 w całym korpusie
corpus <- tm_map(
  corpus,
  content_transformer(function(x) iconv(x, to = "UTF-8", sub = "byte"))
)

# Funkcja do zamiany wzorca na spacje
toSpace <- content_transformer(function(x, pattern) gsub(pattern, " ", x))

# Usuwanie zbędnych znaków i pozostałości
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "@")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "@\\w+")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "\\|")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "[ \t]{2,}")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "(s?)(f|ht)tp(s?)://\\S+\\b")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "http\\w*")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "/")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "(RT|via)((?:\\b\\W*@\\w+)+)")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "www")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "~")
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, toSpace, "â€“")

# Podgląd po częściowym czyszczeniu
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "Yuichi is searching for a man who pushed his son, in the first-class compartment of the bullet train, but he finds Prince instead and is tasered by her."                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
## [2] ""                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
## [3] "Ladybug steals the briefcase from Lemon and Tangerine but is attacked by another assassin (this time from Mexico), the \"Wolf\" (Benito A. Martínez Ocasio) (a Mexican assassin and former kingpin of a drug cartel), who blames Ladybug for fatally poisoning his entire wedding party, including his newlywed wife (whom Ladybug had actually saved). Ladybug was about to get off the train at the next station, but was met by Wolf at the door, who attacks Ladybug. Ladybug doesn't recognize the Wolf and denies having poisoned his entire wedding party. But Ladybug was present at the party as a waiter."
# Czyszczenie podstawowe
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, content_transformer(tolower))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeNumbers)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removeWords, stopwords("english"))
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, removePunctuation)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)

# Podgląd po czyszczeniu
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "yuichi searching man pushed son firstclass compartment bullet train finds prince instead tasered "                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
## [2] ""                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 
## [3] "ladybug steals briefcase lemon tangerine attacked another assassin time mexico wolf benito martínez ocasio mexican assassin former kingpin drug cartel blames ladybug fatally poisoning entire wedding party including newlywed wife ladybug actually saved ladybug get train next station met wolf door attacks ladybug ladybug recognize wolf denies poisoned entire wedding party ladybug present party waiter"
# Usunięcie ewentualnych nazw własnych / słów niepożądanych
corpus <- tm_map(
  corpus,
  removeWords,
  c(
    "rose", "roses", "kate", "kates", "iris", "tyler", "tylers",
    "javi", "javis", "reed", "josh", "joshs", "elliot", "elliots",
    "julian", "julians", "patrick", "patricks", "margot", "margots",
    "one", "however", "ladybug"
  )
)
corpus <- tm_map(corpus, stripWhitespace)

# Podgląd po usunięciu słów niepożądanych
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "yuichi searching man pushed son firstclass compartment bullet train finds prince instead tasered "                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
## [2] ""                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
## [3] " steals briefcase lemon tangerine attacked another assassin time mexico wolf benito martínez ocasio mexican assassin former kingpin drug cartel blames fatally poisoning entire wedding party including newlywed wife actually saved get train next station met wolf door attacks recognize wolf denies poisoned entire wedding party present party waiter"

3.2 Stemming

# Zachowaj kopię korpusu do uzupełniania rdzeni
corpus_copy <- corpus

# Wykonaj stemming
corpus_stemmed <- tm_map(corpus, stemDocument)

# Porównanie przed i po stemmingu
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "yuichi searching man pushed son firstclass compartment bullet train finds prince instead tasered "                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
## [2] ""                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          
## [3] " steals briefcase lemon tangerine attacked another assassin time mexico wolf benito martínez ocasio mexican assassin former kingpin drug cartel blames fatally poisoning entire wedding party including newlywed wife actually saved get train next station met wolf door attacks recognize wolf denies poisoned entire wedding party present party waiter"
corpus_stemmed[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "yuichi search man push son firstclass compart bullet train find princ instead taser"                                                                                                                                                                                                                               
## [2] ""                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  
## [3] "steal briefcas lemon tangerin attack anoth assassin time mexico wolf benito martínez ocasio mexican assassin former kingpin drug cartel blame fatal poison entir wed parti includ newlyw wife actual save get train next station met wolf door attack recogn wolf deni poison entir wed parti present parti waiter"

3.3 Uzupełnienie rdzeni słów po stemmingu

complete_stems <- content_transformer(function(x, dict) {
  x <- unlist(strsplit(x, " "))
  x <- stemCompletion(x, dictionary = corpus_copy, type = "longest")
  paste(x, collapse = " ")
})

corpus_completed <- tm_map(corpus_stemmed, complete_stems, dict = corpus_copy)
corpus_completed <- tm_map(corpus_completed, toSpace, "NA")
corpus_completed <- tm_map(corpus_completed, stripWhitespace)

# Podgląd
corpus_completed[[1]][[1]][1]

# Porównanie obiektów
corpus[[1]][[1]][7:9]
## [1] "yuichi searching man pushed son firstclass compartment bullet train finds prince instead tasered "                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         
## [2] ""

## [2] ""

3.4 Decyzja dotycząca korpusu

# Do dalszej analizy wykorzystujemy corpus_completed

4 Tokenizacja i macierze częstości

4.1 TermDocumentMatrix

tdm <- TermDocumentMatrix(corpus_completed)
tdm
## <<TermDocumentMatrix (terms: 1169, documents: 8)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 1913/7439
## Sparsity           : 80%
## Maximal term length: 16
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
inspect(tdm)

dtm <- DocumentTermMatrix(corpus_completed)
dtm

dtm_m[1:5, 1:5]
##                                   Terms
## Docs                               aaron abandoning abdominal ablaze aboard
##   Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt         1          0         0      0      1
##   Action__Twisters2024.txt             0          2         0      1      0
##   Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt            0          0         0      1      0
##   Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt     0          0         0      0      0

5 Zliczanie częstości słów

# Częstości na podstawie TDM
v <- sort(rowSums(tdm_m), decreasing = TRUE)
tdm_df <- data.frame(word = names(v), freq = v)
head(tdm_df, 10)
# Częstości na podstawie DTM
v2 <- sort(colSums(dtm_m), decreasing = TRUE)
dtm_df <- data.frame(word = names(v2), freq = v2)
head(dtm_df, 10)

6 Eksploracyjna analiza danych

6.1 Chmura słów globalna

wordcloud(
  words = tdm_df$word,
  freq = tdm_df$freq,
  min.freq = 7,
  colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")
)

6.2 Top 10 najczęstszych słów

print(head(tdm_df, 10))
##                    word freq
## deaths           deaths   44
## killing         killing   36
## white             white   29
## tornadoes     tornadoes   28
## realization realization   22
## mothers         mothers   21
## returning     returning   21
## pools             pools   18
## finding         finding   17
## house             house   17

7 Inżynieria cech w modelu Bag of Words

7.1 Reprezentacja słów i dokumentów w przestrzeni wektorowej

dtm
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 8, terms: 1169)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 1913/7439
## Sparsity           : 80%
## Maximal term length: 16
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
inspect(dtm)
## <<DocumentTermMatrix (documents: 8, terms: 1169)>>
## Non-/sparse entries: 1913/7439
## Sparsity           : 80%
## Maximal term length: 16
## Weighting          : term frequency (tf)
## Sample             :
##                                     Terms
## Docs                                 deaths finding house killing mothers pools
##   Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt           33       3     0      16       0     0
##   Action__Twisters2024.txt                3       1     0       1       1     1
##   Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt               1       1     1       4       1     0
##   Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt        0       1     4       1       1    17
##   Supernatural__Smile2022.txt             5       4     5       5       7     0
##   Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt         0       1     3       2       0     0
##   Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt      1       5     0       0      11     0

##   Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt             1         1         0     0
##   Supernatural__Smile2022.txt                  8         2         0     0
##   Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt              5         1         0     0
##   Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt           4         8         0     0
##   Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt            0         3         0     0
dtm_m[1:5, 1:5]
##                                   Terms
## Docs                               aaron abandoning abdominal ablaze aboard
##   Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt         1          0         0      0      1
##   Action__Twisters2024.txt             0          2         0      1      0
##   Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt            0          0         0      1      0
##   Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt     0          0         0      0      0
##   Supernatural__Smile2022.txt          0          2         0      0      0

8 Uczenie maszynowe nienadzorowane

8.1 Dobór liczby klastrów metodą sylwetki

fviz_nbclust(t(dtm_m), kmeans, method = "silhouette") +
  labs(title = "Dobór liczby klastrów", subtitle = "Metoda sylwetki")

8.2 Klastrowanie k-means dla k = 2

set.seed(123)
k <- 2
klastrowanie <- kmeans(dtm_m, centers = k)

8.2.1 Wizualizacja klastrów

fviz_cluster(
  list(data = dtm_m, cluster = klastrowanie$cluster),
  geom = "point",
  main = "Wizualizacja klastrów dokumentów"
)

8.2.2 Tabela podsumowująca klastry

cluster_info <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- sort(colSums(cluster_docs), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_words <- paste(names(word_freq)[1:5], collapse = ", ")
  
  data.frame(
    Klaster = as.integer(i),
    Liczba_dokumentów = length(cluster_docs_idx),
    Top_5_słów = top_words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

cluster_info_df <- do.call(rbind, cluster_info)
cluster_info_df$Klaster <- as.integer(cluster_info_df$Klaster)

document_names <- names(corpus)

documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = as.integer(klastrowanie$cluster),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

documents_clusters$Klaster <- as.integer(as.character(documents_clusters$Klaster))
cluster_info_df$Klaster <- as.integer(cluster_info_df$Klaster)

documents_clusters_z_info <- left_join(
  documents_clusters,
  cluster_info_df,
  by = "Klaster"
)

datatable(
  documents_clusters_z_info,
  caption = "Dokumenty, klastry, najczęstsze słowa i liczebność klastrów",
  rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(pageLength = 10)
)

8.2.3 Chmury słów dla klastrów

for (i in 1:k) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- colSums(cluster_docs)
  
  wordcloud(
    names(word_freq),
    freq = word_freq,
    max.words = 15,
    colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")
  )
  title(paste("Chmura słów - Klaster", i))
}

8.2.4 Przypisanie dokumentów do klastrów

document_names <- names(corpus)
clusters <- klastrowanie$cluster

documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = as.factor(clusters)
)

print(documents_clusters)
##                                                              Dokument Klaster
## Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt             Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt       1
## Action__Twisters2024.txt                     Action__Twisters2024.txt       2
## Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt                   Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt       2
## Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt     Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt       2
## Supernatural__Smile2022.txt               Supernatural__Smile2022.txt       2
## Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt       Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt       2
## Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt       2
## Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt   Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt       2
ggplot(documents_clusters, aes(x = reorder(Dokument, Klaster), fill = Klaster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Przypisanie dokumentów do klastrów",
    x = "Dokument",
    y = "Liczba wystąpień (powinna wynosić 1)",
    fill = "Klaster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

8.3 Klastrowanie k-means dla k = 3

k <- 3
klastrowanie <- kmeans(dtm_m, centers = k)

8.3.1 Wizualizacja klastrów

fviz_cluster(
  list(data = dtm_m, cluster = klastrowanie$cluster),
  geom = "point",
  main = "Wizualizacja klastrów dokumentów"
)

8.3.2 Tabela podsumowująca klastry

cluster_info <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- sort(colSums(cluster_docs), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_words <- paste(names(word_freq)[1:5], collapse = ", ")
  
  data.frame(
    Klaster = as.integer(i),
    Liczba_dokumentów = length(cluster_docs_idx),
    Top_5_słów = top_words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

cluster_info_df <- do.call(rbind, cluster_info)
cluster_info_df$Klaster <- as.integer(cluster_info_df$Klaster)

document_names <- names(corpus)

documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = as.integer(klastrowanie$cluster),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

documents_clusters$Klaster <- as.integer(as.character(documents_clusters$Klaster))
cluster_info_df$Klaster <- as.integer(cluster_info_df$Klaster)

documents_clusters_z_info <- left_join(
  documents_clusters,
  cluster_info_df,
  by = "Klaster"
)

datatable(
  documents_clusters_z_info,
  caption = "Dokumenty, klastry, najczęstsze słowa i liczebność klastrów",
  rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(pageLength = 10)
)

8.3.3 Chmury słów dla klastrów

for (i in 1:k) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- colSums(cluster_docs)
  
  wordcloud(
    names(word_freq),
    freq = word_freq,
    max.words = 15,
    colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")
  )
  title(paste("Chmura słów - Klaster", i))
}

8.3.4 Przypisanie dokumentów do klastrów

document_names <- names(corpus)
clusters <- klastrowanie$cluster

documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = as.factor(clusters)
)

print(documents_clusters)
##                                                              Dokument Klaster
## Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt             Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt       3
## Action__Twisters2024.txt                     Action__Twisters2024.txt       2
## Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt                   Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt       2
## Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt     Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt       2
## Supernatural__Smile2022.txt               Supernatural__Smile2022.txt       1
## Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt       Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt       2
## Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt       2
## Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt   Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt       2
ggplot(documents_clusters, aes(x = reorder(Dokument, Klaster), fill = Klaster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Przypisanie dokumentów do klastrów",
    x = "Dokument",
    y = "Liczba wystąpień (powinna wynosić 1)",
    fill = "Klaster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

8.4 Klastrowanie k-means dla k = 4

k <- 4
klastrowanie <- kmeans(dtm_m, centers = k)

8.4.1 Wizualizacja klastrów

fviz_cluster(
  list(data = dtm_m, cluster = klastrowanie$cluster),
  geom = "point",
  main = "Wizualizacja klastrów dokumentów"
)

8.4.2 Tabela podsumowująca klastry

cluster_info <- lapply(1:k, function(i) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- sort(colSums(cluster_docs), decreasing = TRUE)
  top_words <- paste(names(word_freq)[1:5], collapse = ", ")
  
  data.frame(
    Klaster = as.integer(i),
    Liczba_dokumentów = length(cluster_docs_idx),
    Top_5_słów = top_words,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
})

cluster_info_df <- do.call(rbind, cluster_info)
cluster_info_df$Klaster <- as.integer(cluster_info_df$Klaster)

document_names <- names(corpus)

documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = as.integer(klastrowanie$cluster),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

documents_clusters$Klaster <- as.integer(as.character(documents_clusters$Klaster))
cluster_info_df$Klaster <- as.integer(cluster_info_df$Klaster)

documents_clusters_z_info <- left_join(
  documents_clusters,
  cluster_info_df,
  by = "Klaster"
)

datatable(
  documents_clusters_z_info,
  caption = "Dokumenty, klastry, najczęstsze słowa i liczebność klastrów",
  rownames = FALSE,
  options = list(pageLength = 10)
)

8.4.3 Chmury słów dla klastrów

for (i in 1:k) {
  cluster_docs_idx <- which(klastrowanie$cluster == i)
  cluster_docs <- dtm_m[cluster_docs_idx, , drop = FALSE]
  word_freq <- colSums(cluster_docs)
  
  wordcloud(
    names(word_freq),
    freq = word_freq,
    max.words = 15,
    colors = brewer.pal(8, "Dark2")
  )
  title(paste("Chmura słów - Klaster", i))
}

8.4.4 Przypisanie dokumentów do klastrów

document_names <- names(corpus)
clusters <- klastrowanie$cluster

documents_clusters <- data.frame(
  Dokument = document_names,
  Klaster = as.factor(clusters)
)

print(documents_clusters)
##                                                              Dokument Klaster
## Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt             Action__Bullet_Train2022.txt       2
## Action__Twisters2024.txt                     Action__Twisters2024.txt       1
## Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt                   Dark_Comedy__Menu2022.txt       4
## Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt     Supernatural__Night_Swim2024.txt       4
## Supernatural__Smile2022.txt               Supernatural__Smile2022.txt       3
## Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt       Thriller_Psych__Heretic2024.txt       4
## Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt Thriller_SciFi__Caddo_Lake2024.txt       4
## Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt   Thriller_SciFi__Companion2025.txt       4
ggplot(documents_clusters, aes(x = reorder(Dokument, Klaster), fill = Klaster)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "count", width = 0.7) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Przypisanie dokumentów do klastrów",
    x = "Dokument",
    y = "Liczba wystąpień (powinna wynosić 1)",
    fill = "Klaster"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)
